3.why: We need sizes to wire the chain and the dummy.
3.invariant: sz is the number of real nodes.

4.why: nextp[i] holds the id node i points to.

5.happening: Loop over every real node to wire the chain.
5.why: Each node links to the next.

6.happening: Node {i} is not the tail — point it forward.
6.why: Interior nodes link to the next.
6.invariant: Only the tail ends the list.

6.why: The last node points at nothing.

10.why: It parallels the nodes and adds the dummy value.
10.invariant: disp_vals[i] is node i's shown value.

11.happening: Copy each real node's value for display.

13.why: The dummy is drawn before the head.
13.invariant: The last display slot is the dummy.

14.happening: The dummy's id ({dummy}) sits just past the real nodes.
14.why: A dummy before the head simplifies removing the first node.
14.invariant: dummy is a stable sentinel id.

15.happening: Point the dummy at the head (node 0).
15.why: The dummy leads into the real list.

16.why: It flashes the link we rewire.
16.invariant: changed holds the last rewired node id.

18.happening: Start curr at the head to count.
18.why: We walk the whole list first.

19.why: We need the total length before locating the target.
19.invariant: length equals the nodes counted so far.

19.happening: Reached the end — length is final ({length}).

21.happening: Advance curr to the next node.

22.happening: Reset curr to the dummy for pass two.
22.why: We now locate the node before the target.

23.happening: The target sits length - n = {steps} steps past the dummy.
23.why: Removing the nth-from-end means stopping just before it.
23.invariant: steps is the walk length for pass two.

25.happening: More steps to walk — advance curr.
25.why: curr must land just before the target.
25.invariant: curr is moved nodes past the dummy.

25.happening: curr is now just before the target node.
25.invariant: curr.next is the node to remove.

28.happening: removed = the node right after curr — the one to delete.
28.why: That's the nth node from the end.

29.happening: Unlink it: curr's next skips over the removed node.
29.why: Bypassing the node removes it from the list.
29.invariant: curr now points past the removed node.

31.happening: The new head is whatever the dummy points to.
31.why: If the head itself was removed, this reflects it.
31.invariant: head is the first remaining node (or -1).

32.happening: Start an empty list to read out the result.
32.invariant: order will hold the remaining values.

33.happening: Begin the read-out at the head.

34.invariant: order holds the values read so far.

34.happening: Reached the end — the result is complete.

36.why: Move to the next remaining node.

37.why: The nth-from-end node has been removed.

init.happening: Building the chain, the dummy sentinel, and the counters.
init.why: We need the list and a dummy before counting.
init.invariant: The dummy leads into the list.

loop.happening: Counting the length, walking to the target, or reading out.
loop.why: The brute makes two passes then a read-out.

move.happening: Advancing curr (or the read-out cursor) one node.
move.why: The passes walk node by node.

update.happening: Counting, computing steps, or unlinking the target node.
update.why: The removal is a single next-pointer rewrite.
